one_time_description: Additional COVID-19 funding received from Indigenous Services Canada and other sources for public health measures, income assistance, and reopening costs
economic_notes: Sturgeon Lake Resources Ltd. fully consolidated; Sturgeon Lake Developments Co. Ltd. accounted for by modified equity method. Nation received $10,466,824 in COVID-19 additional funding in 2022.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022). Sturgeon Lake Resources Ltd. is fully consolidated; Sturgeon Lake Developments Co. Ltd. is a GBE accounted for by modified equity method. Prior period restatement in 2021 due to overaccrual of accounts payable in Sturgeon Lake Resources Ltd. ($83,292). No budget was prepared due to COVID-19 State of Emergency. Total government transfers estimated as WCTC ($17,027,548) + ISC Health ($3,347,485) + Government of Alberta ($110,000) + CMHC ($966,232) = $21,451,265. Obligations under capital lease of $1,520,547 included in long-term debt category on balance sheet. No schedule of remuneration included in the provided financial statement text.
